connectShopifyStore.title: How to connect your Shopify store
connectShopifyStore.body:
To start using DisputeDesk, you need to connect your Shopify store. Here's how:

1. From the portal, click "Connect Shopify Store" on the store selection page.
2. Enter your store's domain (e.g. my-store.myshopify.com).
3. You'll be redirected to Shopify to authorize DisputeDesk.
4. Grant the requested permissions — DisputeDesk needs read access to orders, disputes, fulfillments, and customer data.
5. Once authorized, you'll be redirected back and your store will appear in the dashboard.

DisputeDesk accesses your data in read-only mode. Your store data is encrypted and never shared with third parties.

After connecting, DisputeDesk will automatically sync your open disputes. This usually takes a few seconds.

understandingDashboard.title: Understanding the dashboard
understandingDashboard.body:
The dashboard gives you an at-a-glance overview of your dispute operations.

Key metrics displayed:
- Open Disputes: chargebacks currently awaiting a response.
- Win Rate: percentage of disputes resolved in your favor.
- Auto-Saved: evidence packs automatically saved to Shopify this month.
- Resolved: total disputes closed (won or lost).

The Automation Status card shows your current settings: whether auto-build and auto-save are enabled, the minimum completeness score, and whether the blocker gate is active.

The Recent Disputes table lists your latest chargebacks with their status, reason, amount, and deadline. Click any dispute to see full details and manage evidence.

firstDisputeSync.title: Your first dispute sync
firstDisputeSync.body:
After connecting your store, DisputeDesk syncs your open disputes automatically.

How syncing works:
- DisputeDesk checks Shopify for new and updated disputes via the Shopify GraphQL API.
- Disputes are imported with their reason, amount, status, due date, and linked order information.
- Syncing happens automatically via webhooks when Shopify notifies us of changes.
- You can also trigger a manual sync by clicking "Sync Now" on the Disputes page.

After the first sync, if you have automation enabled, DisputeDesk will start evaluating rules and building evidence packs automatically for any disputes that need a response.

automationOverview.title: How automation works
automationOverview.body:
DisputeDesk is automation-first. Here's the flow:

1. A new dispute appears in Shopify.
2. DisputeDesk detects it (via webhook or scheduled sync).
3. Your rules are evaluated to decide: auto-pack or send to review queue.
4. If auto-pack: an evidence pack is built automatically from your order data, tracking info, and store policies.
5. The completeness score is calculated. If it meets your threshold and there are no blockers, the evidence is saved to Shopify automatically.
6. If review is required: the pack appears in your Review Queue for manual approval.

You control every step through your automation settings and rules. Automation can be fully enabled, partially enabled (auto-build but manual save), or disabled entirely.

viewingFilteringDisputes.title: Viewing and filtering disputes
viewingFilteringDisputes.body:
The Disputes page shows all your chargebacks in a sortable table.

You can filter by:
- Status: Needs response, Under review, Won, Lost.
- Search: type keywords to find specific disputes by ID, order number, or reason.

The table shows each dispute's ID, linked order, amount, reason, status, deadline, and when it was last synced.

Two tabs are available:
- All Disputes: every dispute regardless of status.
- Review Queue: only disputes flagged for manual review (based on your rules).

Click any dispute row to open the detail page where you can view evidence packs, generate new packs, or re-sync from Shopify.

syncingDisputes.title: Syncing disputes from Shopify
syncingDisputes.body:
DisputeDesk keeps your disputes up to date through two mechanisms:

1. Webhooks: Shopify sends real-time notifications when disputes are created or updated. DisputeDesk processes these instantly.
2. Manual sync: Click "Sync Now" on the Disputes page to trigger an immediate sync. This is useful if you suspect a webhook was missed.

During a sync, DisputeDesk:
- Fetches all open disputes from Shopify.
- Creates new records for any disputes not yet tracked.
- Updates existing disputes with the latest status and information.
- For new disputes, evaluates your rules and triggers automation if applicable.

Sync status is shown with a "Last synced" timestamp on each dispute.

reviewQueue.title: The review queue
reviewQueue.body:
The Review Queue contains disputes that require your manual review before evidence is saved to Shopify.

Disputes end up in the review queue when:
- A rule routes them to review (e.g. high-value disputes over a certain amount).
- No rule matches and the default action is review.
- You have "Require review before auto-save" enabled in your automation settings.

From the Review Queue tab on the Disputes page, you can:
- See all disputes awaiting your attention.
- Click into any dispute to review the evidence pack.
- Click "Approve" to trigger the automation pipeline (pack building and saving).

This gives you full control over which disputes get automated responses and which ones you want to handle personally.

approvingDisputes.title: Approving disputes from the review queue
approvingDisputes.body:
When a dispute is in your Review Queue, you need to approve it before DisputeDesk will build and save evidence.

To approve a dispute:
1. Go to the Disputes page and switch to the "Review Queue" tab.
2. Click the "Approve" button next to the dispute.
3. DisputeDesk will immediately trigger the automation pipeline.
4. An evidence pack will be built from your order data, tracking, and policies.
5. If the pack passes your completeness and blocker gates, evidence will be saved to Shopify.

You can also approve from the dispute detail page by clicking "Approve & Save."

All approvals are logged in the audit trail for compliance tracking.

howPacksBuilt.title: How evidence packs are built automatically
howPacksBuilt.body:
When DisputeDesk builds an evidence pack, it automatically collects evidence from multiple sources:

- Order data: line items, amounts, billing address (city-level only — PII is redacted), customer tenure, and refund history.
- Fulfillment & tracking: carrier name, tracking numbers, tracking URLs, and delivery status.
- Store policies: snapshots of your shipping, refund, terms, and cancellation policies.

The pack is structured by dispute reason. For example, a "Product not received" dispute will prioritize shipping and tracking evidence, while a "Fraudulent" dispute will emphasize order and customer verification.

After collection, DisputeDesk calculates a completeness score and identifies any blockers (missing required evidence). You can then upload additional files to strengthen the pack.

generatingPackManually.title: Generating a pack manually
generatingPackManually.body:
You can manually generate an evidence pack for any dispute:

1. Go to the dispute detail page.
2. Click "Generate Pack" (or "Generate New Pack" if a pack already exists).
3. DisputeDesk will collect evidence from your order, tracking, and policy data.
4. The pack will appear on the page once building is complete (usually a few seconds).

Manual generation is useful when:
- Automation is disabled and you want to create packs on demand.
- You want to regenerate a pack after uploading new evidence.
- A previous pack failed and you want to retry.

Note: pack generation counts against your monthly quota. Check your usage on the Billing page.

completenessScore.title: Understanding the completeness score
completenessScore.body:
Every evidence pack has a completeness score from 0% to 100%. This score indicates how much of the recommended evidence has been collected.

How it works:
- Each dispute reason has a template of expected evidence items (e.g. tracking info, delivery proof, policy snapshots).
- DisputeDesk checks which items are present in the pack.
- Required items contribute more weight than optional ones.
- The score is the weighted percentage of collected vs. expected items.

Scoring thresholds:
- 80%+ (green): strong pack, ready for auto-save.
- 60–79% (yellow): acceptable but could be stronger.
- Below 60% (red): weak pack — consider uploading more evidence.

If you have auto-save enabled, packs below your configured minimum score will not be saved automatically. You can adjust this threshold in Settings > Automation.

fixingBlockers.title: Fixing blockers in your evidence pack
fixingBlockers.body:
Blockers are missing required evidence items that prevent an evidence pack from being auto-saved to Shopify.

Common blockers include:
- Missing tracking information for "Product not received" disputes.
- Missing refund policy for "Credit not processed" disputes.
- Missing order details when order data could not be fetched.

To fix blockers:
1. Open the evidence pack from the dispute detail page.
2. Check the "Evidence Checklist" section — blockers are highlighted in red.
3. Upload the missing evidence using the upload section.
4. After uploading, the pack will re-evaluate and update the blocker status.

If you have "Enforce zero blockers" enabled in automation settings, packs with any remaining blockers will not be auto-saved. You can disable this gate if you prefer to save packs even with missing items.

uploadingEvidence.title: Uploading additional evidence
uploadingEvidence.body:
You can upload files to strengthen your evidence pack:

1. Open the evidence pack detail page.
2. Scroll to the "Upload Evidence" section.
3. Click to upload or drag and drop files.
4. Supported formats: images (JPG, PNG) and PDF files.
5. Maximum file size: 10 MB per file.

Uploaded files are attached to the evidence pack and included when saving to Shopify. They appear in the "Evidence Items" section with a timestamp and source label.

Common uploads include:
- Screenshots of customer communication.
- Delivery confirmation photos.
- Signed receipts or invoices.
- Additional tracking documentation.

evidenceChecklist.title: Evidence checklist explained
evidenceChecklist.body:
The Evidence Checklist shows what evidence has been collected and what's still missing.

Each item in the checklist is marked as:
- Collected (green check): this evidence has been found and included.
- Required but missing (red, labeled "required"): this is a blocker — you need to provide this evidence.
- Recommended (yellow): not strictly required, but providing it would strengthen your case.

The checklist is tailored to each dispute's reason code. For example:
- Product Not Received: prioritizes tracking, delivery proof, and shipping policy.
- Fraudulent: prioritizes order verification, customer details, and AVS/CVV match.
- Credit Not Processed: prioritizes refund records and refund policy.

Focusing on required items first will improve your completeness score and remove blockers that prevent auto-saving.

configuringAutomation.title: Configuring automation settings
configuringAutomation.body:
Automation settings control how DisputeDesk handles new disputes. Access them from Settings > Automation.

Available toggles:
- Auto-build evidence packs: automatically generate packs when new disputes are detected.
- Auto-save evidence to Shopify: automatically push complete evidence to Shopify when quality gates pass.
- Require review before auto-save: send packs to the review queue for manual approval first.
- Enforce zero blockers: prevent auto-save if any required evidence items are missing.

Minimum completeness score: set the threshold (0–100) below which packs will not be auto-saved. The default is 80.

Recommended configurations:
- Full automation: auto-build ON, auto-save ON, review OFF, score 80.
- Review-first: auto-build ON, auto-save ON, review ON, score 60.
- Manual only: auto-build OFF, auto-save OFF — you generate and save packs yourself.

creatingFirstRule.title: Creating your first rule
creatingFirstRule.body:
Rules let you control how disputes are routed — either to auto-pack or to the review queue.

To create a rule:
1. Go to the Rules page.
2. Click "Add Rule."
3. Fill in the details:
   - Name: a label for your reference (e.g. "High-value review").
   - Match Reasons: select which dispute reasons this rule applies to (leave empty to match all).
   - Amount range: set a minimum and/or maximum dispute amount.
   - Action: choose "Auto-Pack" (automatic processing) or "Send to Review" (manual queue).
4. Click "Create Rule."

Example: create a rule that sends all disputes over $500 to review:
- Name: "High-value review"
- Min Amount: 500
- Action: Send to Review

Rules are available on Starter and Pro plans. Free plan users can upgrade from the Billing page.

rulePriority.title: Rule priority and evaluation order
rulePriority.body:
Rules are evaluated top to bottom. The first rule that matches a dispute wins — no further rules are checked.

How matching works:
- If a rule specifies match reasons, the dispute must have one of those reasons.
- If a rule specifies an amount range, the dispute amount must fall within that range.
- If a rule has no conditions (empty reasons, no amount range), it matches all disputes.

To reorder rules, drag and drop them on the Rules page. Put your most specific rules at the top and catch-all rules at the bottom.

If no rule matches a dispute, it goes to the Review Queue by default.

Tip: create a catch-all "Auto-Pack" rule at the bottom to automate everything, and put specific "Send to Review" rules above it for high-value or unusual disputes.

completenessBlockerGates.title: Completeness and blocker gates
completenessBlockerGates.body:
Gates are quality checks that control whether an evidence pack can be auto-saved to Shopify.

Two gates are available:

1. Completeness gate: the pack's completeness score must meet your configured minimum threshold (default: 80%). Packs below this score will not be auto-saved.

2. Blocker gate: if "Enforce zero blockers" is enabled, any pack with missing required evidence items will not be auto-saved.

Both gates must pass for auto-save to proceed. If either gate fails, the pack stays in its current state and you can:
- Upload additional evidence to improve the score.
- Manually review and save the pack if you're satisfied with the evidence.
- Adjust your gate thresholds in Settings > Automation.

Gates only affect auto-save. You can always save evidence to Shopify manually regardless of gate status.

planComparison.title: Comparing Free, Starter, and Pro plans
planComparison.body:
DisputeDesk offers three plans:

Free ($0/month):
- 3 evidence packs per month.
- Manual pack generation only.
- PDF export.
- Save evidence to Shopify.

Starter ($29/month):
- 50 evidence packs per month.
- Auto-pack on dispute sync.
- Custom routing rules.
- PDF export and save to Shopify.
- 7-day free trial.

Pro ($79/month):
- Unlimited evidence packs.
- Auto-pack on dispute sync.
- Custom routing rules.
- Priority support.
- PDF export and save to Shopify.
- 7-day free trial.

All plans include the core features: evidence collection, completeness scoring, PDF export, and saving to Shopify. Paid plans add automation, rules, and higher quotas.

upgradingPlan.title: How to upgrade your plan
upgradingPlan.body:
To upgrade your DisputeDesk plan:

1. Go to the Billing page.
2. Review the plan comparison table.
3. Click "Upgrade to [Plan]" on the plan you want.
4. You'll be redirected to Shopify to approve the recurring charge.
5. After approval, your plan is activated immediately.

Paid plans include a 7-day free trial. You won't be charged until the trial period ends.

If you need to downgrade, contact support. Downgrades take effect at the end of your current billing cycle, and your data is always retained.

Your plan determines your monthly pack quota and which features are available (auto-pack, custom rules, priority support).

packLimits.title: Understanding pack limits and quotas
packLimits.body:
Each plan has a monthly limit on evidence packs:

- Free: 3 packs per month.
- Starter: 50 packs per month.
- Pro: Unlimited.

The counter resets on the first of each month. You can check your current usage on the Billing page under "Usage This Month."

When you reach your limit:
- New pack generation (manual or automatic) will be blocked.
- A warning banner will appear on the dispute detail page.
- You'll see an "Upgrade Plan" button to increase your quota.

Existing packs are not affected — you can still view, download PDFs, and save completed packs to Shopify even after hitting your limit.

trialPeriod.title: Free trial details
trialPeriod.body:
Both the Starter and Pro plans include a 7-day free trial.

During the trial:
- You have full access to all features of the plan.
- Your pack quota matches the plan level.
- No charge is applied until the trial ends.

After the trial:
- If you keep the plan, billing starts automatically through Shopify.
- If you cancel before the trial ends, you return to the Free plan with no charge.

Trials are managed through Shopify's billing system. You can view your billing status in Shopify Admin > Settings > Billing.

howEvidenceSaved.title: How evidence is saved to Shopify
howEvidenceSaved.body:
When you save evidence, DisputeDesk pushes structured data to Shopify using the disputeEvidenceUpdate GraphQL API.

What gets saved:
- Shipping and tracking documentation (carrier, tracking number, delivery status).
- Customer communication and order details.
- Refund and cancellation policy text.
- Additional file attachments you uploaded.

Each section of your evidence pack is mapped to the appropriate Shopify evidence field. DisputeDesk handles the formatting and field mapping automatically.

After saving:
- The pack status changes to "Saved to Shopify."
- A deep link to Shopify Admin appears so you can review the evidence there.
- An audit event is logged for compliance tracking.

Important: saving evidence to Shopify is NOT the same as submitting to the card network. See "What happens after saving" for next steps.

fieldMapping.title: How evidence fields are mapped to Shopify
fieldMapping.body:
DisputeDesk maps your evidence pack sections to Shopify's dispute evidence fields:

- Shipping & Tracking → shippingDocumentation: carrier info, tracking numbers, delivery proof.
- Customer Details → customerCommunication: customer email, tenure, order history context.
- Refund Policy → refundPolicyDisclosure: your store's refund policy text.
- Cancellation Policy → cancellationPolicyDisclosure: your cancellation terms.
- Order Information → accessActivityLog: order details, line items, amounts.
- Uploaded Files → uncategorizedText: additional documentation you provided.

This mapping happens automatically when you save evidence. You don't need to manually assign fields.

Shopify uses these structured fields when presenting evidence to the card network. The better your evidence maps to the right fields, the stronger your case.

afterSaving.title: What happens after saving evidence
afterSaving.body:
After DisputeDesk saves your evidence to Shopify, here's what happens next:

1. Evidence appears in Shopify Admin: go to Orders > the linked order > Chargeback to review the evidence Shopify received.
2. Review in Shopify: you can add final notes or make adjustments directly in Shopify Admin.
3. Shopify sends evidence to the card network (Visa, Mastercard, etc.) either:
   - When you click the respond button in Shopify Admin, or
   - Automatically on the dispute's due date if you haven't responded manually.
4. Card network reviews: the card network evaluates the evidence and makes a decision.
5. Resolution: the dispute is resolved as "Won" (reversed in your favor) or "Lost."

DisputeDesk monitors the dispute status and updates it when Shopify reports a resolution.

notSubmitToNetworks.title: DisputeDesk does NOT send evidence to card networks
notSubmitToNetworks.body:
This is an important distinction: DisputeDesk saves evidence to Shopify, but does NOT send it onward to card networks (Visa, Mastercard, American Express, etc.).

Here's the process:
1. DisputeDesk builds your evidence pack and saves it to Shopify via API.
2. Forwarding evidence to the card network is handled entirely by Shopify.
3. Shopify either auto-sends on the due date or you respond manually from Shopify Admin.

Why it works this way:
- Shopify is the merchant of record and handles the official communication with card networks.
- This ensures compliance with payment processor requirements.
- Your evidence is formatted and structured by DisputeDesk, then handed off to Shopify for the official process.

You always maintain control. Review your evidence in Shopify Admin before Shopify forwards it to the card network.

onboarding.bannerDesc: Take a quick 2-minute tour to learn how to manage chargebacks like a pro.
onboarding.welcomeDesc: Let's take a quick tour to help you get started with managing your Shopify chargebacks efficiently. This will only take 2 minutes.
onboarding.dashboardDesc: This is your dashboard where you can see all your key metrics at a glance: active disputes, win rate, evidence packs used, and upcoming deadlines.
onboarding.disputesListDesc: View and manage all your disputes in one place. Filter by status, sort by priority, and quickly see which disputes need your attention.
onboarding.evidencePacksDesc: Create reusable evidence templates for different dispute types. Each pack contains all the documents needed to fight a specific type of chargeback.
onboarding.automationRulesDesc: Set up automation rules to automatically attach evidence packs, send notifications, or flag urgent disputes based on custom conditions.
onboarding.policiesDesc: Upload and manage your store policies (Terms of Service, Refund Policy, etc.) to quickly include them as evidence in disputes.
onboarding.completeDesc: You've seen what DisputeDesk can do. Now connect your real Shopify store to start managing disputes, building evidence packs, and winning chargebacks automatically.

hero.headline: Run chargebacks like an ops team — not a 25% success-fee tax.
hero.subheadline: DisputeDesk helps you build evidence packs fast, enforce completeness, and keep a defensible audit trail — with merchant-controlled workflows and predictable pricing.
hero.bullet1: Evidence packs in minutes — drafts are unlimited, packs count only on export
hero.bullet2: Review queue + completeness gate to prevent weak submissions
hero.bullet3: Immutable audit trail for every change, file, and decision
hero.disclaimer: Evidence is saved to Shopify via API. Submission to the card network happens in Shopify Admin (or auto-submits on due date).

howItWorks.title: How it works
howItWorks.subtitle: Three simple steps to better dispute management
howItWorks.step1Desc: Install from the Shopify App Store. DisputeDesk syncs disputes automatically from that point on.
howItWorks.step2Desc: When a dispute appears, DisputeDesk automatically builds an evidence pack with rules and completeness scoring.
howItWorks.step3Desc: Complete packs are saved to Shopify automatically. Submit in Shopify Admin or let Shopify auto-submit on due date.

security.title: Built for security and compliance
security.subtitle: Your data is protected at every step
security.encryptedDesc: All API keys and access tokens are encrypted at rest with AES-256.
security.auditDesc: Every action is logged with timestamps and user attribution for full traceability.
security.signedDesc: Evidence files are stored with time-limited signed URLs to prevent unauthorized access.

pricing.title: Predictable pricing for dispute ops
pricing.subtitle: Free is for evaluation. Paid plans are for running dispute operations — with clear pack limits and simple top-ups. No hidden percentage.
pricing.trialInfo: Paid plans include a 14-day trial with 25 packs. Free includes 3 packs total (lifetime).
pricing.topUpsDesc: Purchase top-up bundles any time. Credits are added immediately upon payment.

footer.tagline: Your command center for Shopify disputes.
footer.copyright: © 2026 DisputeDesk. Not affiliated with Shopify Inc.

sessionRequired.title: Session Required
sessionRequired.bannerBody: Please open DisputeDesk from Shopify Admin (Apps) so we can verify your store session.
sessionRequired.instructions: This app must be launched from your Shopify Admin panel. If you arrived here by accident, close this tab and open the app from your store's admin dashboard.

packTemplates.title: Evidence Packs Library
packTemplates.subtitle: Manage reusable evidence templates for different dispute types
packTemplates.searchPlaceholder: Search packs by name or type...
packTemplates.emptyDescription: Create your first evidence pack template to get started
packTemplates.modalDescription: Build a reusable template for dispute evidence
packTemplates.descriptionPlaceholder: Describe when this pack should be used and what evidence it contains...
packTemplates.nextSteps: After creating the pack, you'll be able to add documents, configure automatic fields, and set up submission rules.
packTemplates.confirmDelete: Are you sure you want to delete this pack template? This cannot be undone.
